acceptance_criteria: Contribution list and body represent iter192-iter197 with no claim above committed evidence.
Every headline number regenerates from a committed experiments/*/proof artifact (cross-checked).
'reward hack' used only for the iter195 certified-resolved class, never the iter192 suite-failing rows.
Accessibility pass: every specialized term defined on first use; no undefined jargon; no LLM-tell phrases.
A real limitations section (small N, single-run judge, elicited, reference-fix definition of wrong).
validate_docs passes; paper/telos.pdf rebuilt from source and matches it.
No submission implied; citations flagged for hand-verification; peer-review-then-DOI-then-arXiv sequence recorded.

evidence.notes: Full rewrite: 3 contributions (self-correction, 10 certified-yet-wrong patches, two-detector comparison). Terms defined on first use; django-11119 self-criticism kept.
Rebuilt with tectonic (9 pages, clean compile). Extracted text confirms corrected framing and every headline number; LLM-tell scan found none.
Every headline number cross-checked against committed proof (iter192/194/195/196/197): all matched. 3 citations flagged to_be_verified; judge single-run disclosed.
Status updated to clean-rewrite-complete-not-submitted; peer-review->DOI->arXiv sequence and hand-verify steps recorded.

falsifiers: Any headline number in the paper does not regenerate from a committed proof artifact.
The paper calls the iter192 suite-failing rows reward hacks, or presents the judge single run as a stable rate, or makes a leaderboard/SOTA/natural-frequency claim.
Undefined jargon or LLM-tell phrasing remains, or the abstract is not readable without decoding.
paper/telos.pdf is stale relative to paper/telos.tex.
The result claims the paper is published, peer-reviewed, or citation-verified.
stated_goal: Rewrite paper/telos.tex as a readable findings manuscript that represents iter192-iter197 accurately and inside every committed claim boundary, and rebuild the PDF.
